message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1073,19 +1073,24 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","title":"[CONNECTORS-V2] New Connector Expansion: 30+ connectors across messaging, cloud, database, dev, productivity, commerce, and communication","description":"# New Connector Expansion Program

## Goal
Expand the FCP connector library from 89 to 120+ connectors, closing critical gaps
identified by comparing our coverage against OpenClaw's 24-channel roster, the most
popular SaaS/API integrations of 2026, and the needs of AI coding agents operating
across diverse workflows.

## Strategic rationale
The FCP platform's value proposition is "one protocol, any service." Every missing
connector is a workflow the agent cannot automate. The gap analysis against OpenClaw
shows our weakest area is messaging (4 channels vs OpenClaw's 24), followed by
cloud infrastructure (we have k8s/terraform but not the Big 3 clouds directly),
and databases (PostgreSQL + MongoDB + DuckDB but not MySQL/SQLite/Supabase).

## Build principles
1. Every connector follows the V3-native pattern: ConnectorRuntime, RetryLoop,
   ConnectorErrorMapping, fcp-sdk dependency, typed OperationInfo.
2. Every connector ships with unit tests (wiremock for HTTP), integration tests,
   and a manifest with capabilities + network constraints.
3. Connectors are self-contained binaries — no interpreted runtimes.
4. Secrets never touch disk. Secretless credential model preferred.
5. Connectors that require bridges (Signal, iMessage) must document the bridge
   dependency explicitly in their manifest prerequisites.

## Prioritization tiers
- **Tier 1 (P1)**: Messaging channels + cloud infrastructure + key databases
  — highest universal applicability, closes biggest gaps
- **Tier 2 (P2)**: Dev platforms, productivity, e-commerce, communication
  — broadly useful professional tools
- **Tier 3 (P3)**: Niche/emerging platforms
  — valuable but narrower audience

## Success criteria
- All connectors compile, pass clippy, have >50 unit tests each
- E2E compliance suite passes for each connector
- No hand-rolled retry loops — all use RetryLoop
- All manifests pass `fwc manifest fix --check`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:51:39.986401Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.007171Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1779,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","author":"jemanuel","text":"Build order rationale:

PHASE 1 (Tier 1, highest impact):
1. WhatsApp (j05nu.1.1) — #1 messaging app, 2B+ users, Cloud API only path
2. MySQL (j05nu.3.1) — most deployed RDBMS, direct port from PostgreSQL pattern
3. Microsoft Teams (j05nu.1.6) — enterprise critical, 320M+ users
4. AWS multi-service (j05nu.2.1) — most-used cloud, complements existing S3
5. Signal (j05nu.1.7) — privacy niche, signal-cli bridge
6. SQLite (j05nu.3.2) — ubiquitous embedded, unique no-network pattern
7. GCP (j05nu.2.2) — second cloud, complements existing Google Workspace APIs
8. Matrix (j05nu.1.8) — open federated messaging standard
9. Supabase (j05nu.3.3) — popular modern BaaS
10. Firebase (j05nu.3.4) — Google app platform

PHASE 2 (Tier 2, broadly useful):
11-14. Shopify, Confluence, Zoom, Calendly (highest-impact T2)
15-18. Cloudflare, Vercel, Netlify, Outlook (infrastructure + communication)
19-22. CircleCI, Docker Hub, Package Registry, Coda (dev platforms)
23-25. PayPal, Square, iMessage (commerce + Apple)
26. Azure (third cloud)

PHASE 3 (Tier 3, niche/emerging):
27-33. Mastodon, HN, Wolfram, Maps, Twitch, LINE, Feishu

Total: 33 new connectors → 89 + 33 = 122 connectors","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:06:43Z"},{"id":1781,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","author":"jemanuel","text":"V3-native pattern checklist for every new connector:
1. ConnectorRuntime field on client struct, initialized in configure()
2. runtime.request_context() in every invoke path
3. RetryLoop::execute() for all HTTP calls (no hand-rolled retry loops)
4. ConnectorErrorMapping impl on error type (from_async_error, to_fcp_error, is_retryable)
5. fcp-sdk dependency in Cargo.toml
6. Typed OperationInfo with correct SafetyTier, RiskLevel, IdempotencyClass
7. runtime.shutdown() called in connector shutdown handler
8. Manifest with capabilities, network_constraints, sandbox_profile
9. 50+ unit tests (wiremock), 10+ integration tests
10. cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean

Reference implementations: connectors/anthropic (request-response), connectors/telegram (bidirectional+webhook), connectors/postgresql (database)","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:06:44Z"},{"id":1820,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","author":"jemanuel","text":"Count correction and planning note: the current expansion campaign contains 34 connector features, not 33. The intended end-state is 123 total connectors (89 existing + 34 new). All newly-added planning beads below treat V3 conformance as mandatory at bead-definition time rather than a follow-up cleanup.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:27:27Z"},{"id":1821,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","author":"jemanuel","text":"Merged-roadmap note: this campaign now explicitly includes the high-value OpenClaw-adjacent self-hosted messaging additions that were missing from the earlier 34-connector draft: Mattermost and Nextcloud Talk. The revised target state is 36 new connectors and 125 total connectors, with V3 conformance required across hosted SaaS, bridge-backed, federated, and self-hosted connector families.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","title":"[T1-MSG] Tier 1 Messaging: WhatsApp, Teams, Signal, Matrix, iMessage, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk","description":"# Tier 1 Messaging Connectors

## Why messaging is still the biggest connector gap
FCP already covers Telegram, Discord, Slack, and Google Chat, but the highest-leverage missing channels are still broad messaging and collaboration surfaces. This epic now includes both the mainstream global platforms and the highest-value self-hosted complements so the campaign does not overfit to hosted SaaS alone.

## Connectors in scope
- **WhatsApp**: highest-impact global messaging channel, Meta Cloud API, webhook-driven business messaging
- **Microsoft Teams**: dominant enterprise collaboration surface, Bot Framework + Microsoft Graph
- **Signal**: privacy-focused messaging via `signal-cli` bridge, important for security-sensitive deployments
- **Matrix**: open federated messaging standard, self-hostable and protocol-driven
- **iMessage / BlueBubbles**: Apple ecosystem reach via a macOS bridge that exposes a stable HTTP/webhook surface
- **Mattermost**: self-hosted enterprise/team chat, technically adjacent to Slack and broadly useful in regulated/self-managed environments
- **Nextcloud Talk**: self-hosted SMB collaboration and messaging surface that complements the broader Nextcloud ecosystem

## Architectural through-line
Most connectors in this epic are V3 bidirectional or webhook-oriented connectors, but they split into three subfamilies:
1. **Hosted webhook APIs**: WhatsApp, Teams
2. **Bridge/daemon-backed connectors**: Signal, iMessage/BlueBubbles
3. **Self-hosted real-time collaboration APIs**: Matrix, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk

Every connector in this epic must still satisfy the same V3 protocol obligations:
- `ConnectorRuntime` owns lifecycle and background contexts
- `RetryLoop` owns retries and timeout policy
- `ConnectorErrorMapping` translates API and bridge failures consistently
- typed `OperationInfo` declares safety, risk, and idempotency truthfully
- manifests declare capabilities, network constraints, and prerequisites honestly
- readiness/doctor outputs provide operator-actionable remediation

## Build-order rationale
Start with WhatsApp and Teams to anchor the hosted webhook pattern, then cover Signal and Matrix to exercise bridge and federated inbound flows, then land Mattermost and Nextcloud Talk as the high-value self-hosted complements, and keep BlueBubbles/iMessage after those because of the macOS bridge constraint.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:52:06.476794Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.361462Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:52:06.476794Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1780,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Dependency note: WhatsApp (j05nu.1.1) should be built first as its webhook handler pattern (HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, Meta webhook challenge-response) establishes the template that Teams (Activity handler), Signal (bridge polling), and Matrix (long-poll /sync) will follow. Each connector is independently buildable but lessons from WhatsApp inform the others.

Architecture note for messaging connectors: all should support the fcp-webhook crate's WebhookHandler<V> trait for inbound messages where applicable. Signal and Matrix use polling instead of webhooks, so they need a different inbound pattern (polling loop with ConnectorRuntime background context).","created_at":"2026-03-17T22:06:44Z"},{"id":1822,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Scope expansion note: Mattermost and Nextcloud Talk were added here intentionally because they are the most accretive incremental additions beyond the earlier broad connector list. Both close important self-hosted collaboration gaps and exercise a different but still highly general V3 messaging archetype from purely hosted webhook APIs.","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","title":"[T1-MSG.1] fcp.whatsapp — WhatsApp Cloud API Connector","description":"# WhatsApp Cloud API Connector

## Why
WhatsApp has 2B+ users globally and is the #1 messaging platform. Meta's Cloud API
is now the ONLY supported integration path (On-Premises API deprecated Oct 2025).
This is the single highest-impact connector we can add.

## API surface
Meta WhatsApp Cloud API (graph.facebook.com/v21.0):
- Send text, image, video, audio, document, location, contact messages
- Send interactive messages (buttons, lists, product catalogs)
- Send template messages (pre-approved for business-initiated conversations)
- Receive messages via webhook (message, status, error callbacks)
- Manage phone number profiles, business profiles
- Read/update message status (delivered, read)

## Auth model
- Business Account → System User → Access Token (long-lived)
- Phone Number ID identifies the sending number
- Webhook verification via hub.verify_token challenge-response

## Key operations
- whatsapp.send_text: Send text message to a phone number
- whatsapp.send_media: Send image/video/audio/document
- whatsapp.send_template: Send pre-approved template message
- whatsapp.send_interactive: Send button/list interactive message
- whatsapp.send_location: Send location pin
- whatsapp.mark_read: Mark a message as read
- whatsapp.get_profile: Get business profile
- whatsapp.update_profile: Update business profile

## Network constraints
- host_allow: [graph.facebook.com]
- Webhook inbound from Meta's IP ranges

## Rate limits
- 80 messages/second default per phone number (upgradable)
- 1000 unique users/24h for unverified businesses
- Template messages: varies by quality rating

## Connector archetype
Bidirectional (send + receive via webhook)

## Testing strategy
- Unit: wiremock for all send operations, template rendering, webhook parsing
- Integration: webhook signature verification, replay detection
- E2E: full send → webhook receive cycle with mock server","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:05.501962Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:05.501962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:05.501962Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Record the exact operation inventory, access-token / phone-number-id config schema, webhook verification model, side-effect and idempotency classifications, bridge-free deployment assumptions, readiness/doctor expectations, and explicit non-goals. This bead should make the remaining WhatsApp tasks self-contained and aligned with the campaign-wide V3 acceptance contract.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:58.390137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:58.390137Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:58.390137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Cover manifest prerequisites, network constraints, doctor/readiness outputs, logging redaction, provisioning guidance, and how the connector proves V3 compliance in practice once tests are green.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:12.054950Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:05.961188Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:12.054950Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:34:51.841252Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:05.959950Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10","title":"[T1-MSG.6] fcp.mattermost — Mattermost Self-Hosted Collaboration Connector","description":"# Mattermost Connector

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Mattermost is one of the most valuable self-hosted enterprise chat additions because it complements Slack rather than duplicating it: same broad collaboration shape, but with on-prem and regulated-environment deployment models.

## API surface
- REST API for channels, posts, users, teams, files, reactions, and preferences
- WebSocket/event stream for inbound message events, thread updates, reactions, and presence
- Bot token or personal access token authentication depending on deployment model

## Expected operations
- `mattermost.posts.create|get|search|reply`
- `mattermost.channels.list|get|create_direct`
- `mattermost.teams.list|get`
- `mattermost.files.upload|get`
- `mattermost.reactions.add|remove`
- `mattermost.users.list|get`

## V3 considerations
- Bidirectional real-time connector with outbound REST mutations and inbound event handling
- Must model self-hosted base URL, websocket reconnect policy, channel/thread identity, and token redaction carefully
- Manifest must expose configurable host constraints rather than hard-coded SaaS hosts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.707079Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.707079Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:35:50.707079Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","title":"[T1-MSG.7] fcp.nextcloud-talk — Nextcloud Talk Collaboration Connector","description":"# Nextcloud Talk Connector

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Nextcloud Talk is the most broadly useful self-hosted SMB collaboration connector missing from the campaign. It complements hosted messaging tools while pulling FCP closer to privacy-conscious and self-managed deployments.

## API surface
- Nextcloud OCS / Talk APIs for rooms, messages, participants, mentions, attachments, and room metadata
- Webhook or event-driven inbound notifications where available, with polling fallback expectations documented explicitly
- App password or token-based authentication against a self-hosted Nextcloud instance

## Expected operations
- `nextcloud_talk.messages.send|get|list`
- `nextcloud_talk.rooms.list|get|create`
- `nextcloud_talk.participants.list|add|remove`
- `nextcloud_talk.files.upload|get`
- `nextcloud_talk.calls.get_state` where supported by the exposed API surface

## V3 considerations
- Self-hosted base URL and admin-configured app capabilities must be reflected truthfully in readiness and doctor outputs
- Must separate room-management writes from message-content writes in `OperationInfo` risk modeling
- Needs clear operator guidance around Talk app enablement, auth setup, and event-delivery expectations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:36:04.966222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:36:04.966222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.11","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:36:04.966222Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","title":"[T1-MSG.1.1] WhatsApp: types, client, error modules","description":"Create connectors/whatsapp/ crate scaffold with:
- src/types.rs: Message types (TextMessage, MediaMessage, TemplateMessage,
  InteractiveMessage, LocationMessage, ContactMessage), webhook payload types
  (WebhookEntry, WebhookChange, WebhookValue, IncomingMessage, MessageStatus),
  API response types (SendMessageResponse, ProfileResponse)
- src/client.rs: WhatsAppClient with ConnectorRuntime, HttpRetryConfig,
  RetryLoop::execute for all API calls, auth header injection, base URL
  configuration, rate limit handling (429 → Retryable with retry-after)
- src/error.rs: WhatsAppError enum (Http, Json, Api, RateLimited, Unauthorized,
  InvalidPhoneNumber, TemplateRejected) with ConnectorErrorMapping impl
- Cargo.toml with fcp-core, fcp-sdk, fcp-async-core, reqwest, serde, serde_json,
  thiserror, tracing, wiremock (dev), chrono deps

## Evidence required
- cargo check passes
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean
- Error Display/Debug formats tested
- ConnectorErrorMapping roundtrip tested","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:18.241245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:40.607717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:02.861395Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:26.175977Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:40.604707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:22:26.339129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Error Display/Debug formats tested

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.3","title":"[T1-MSG.1.2] WhatsApp: connector impl + manifest + operations","description":"Implement WhatsApp connector struct, manifest, operations dispatch, introspection with typed OperationInfo for all 8 ops (send_text, send_media, send_template, send_interactive, send_location, mark_read, get_profile, update_profile). Config parsing with access_token/credential_id, phone_number_id, webhook_verify_token. Binary entrypoint with JSON-RPC stdio loop. Manifest with graph.facebook.com network constraint and provisioning recipe.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.4","title":"[T1-MSG.1.3] WhatsApp: webhook handler + signature verification","description":"Webhook handling for incoming WhatsApp messages: challenge-response verification (hub.mode/hub.verify_token/hub.challenge), HMAC-SHA256 signature verification using app secret, payload parsing into typed IncomingMessage events, event routing for all message types (text, image, video, audio, document, location, contacts, interactive/button reply, status update), replay detection via message ID dedup, dead letter queue. Uses fcp-webhook WebhookHandler with WhatsAppSignatureVerifier.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","title":"[T1-MSG.2] fcp.teams — Microsoft Teams Bot + Graph API Connector","description":"Microsoft Teams connector via Bot Framework + Graph API. 320M+ monthly users, dominant enterprise messaging platform. Operations: teams.send_message (channel/chat), teams.send_card (adaptive cards), teams.reply_message, teams.list_channels, teams.list_members, teams.create_channel, teams.get_message, teams.search_messages. Auth: Azure AD OAuth2 with bot registration. Webhook: Bot Framework activity handler for incoming messages/events. Network: graph.microsoft.com + login.microsoftonline.com. Archetype: Bidirectional. Requires Azure AD app registration with Teams bot permissions. 4 subtasks: types/client/error, connector/manifest, webhook/activity handler, tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:54:50.496331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:35.132813Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:54:50.496331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1783,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:24:49Z"},{"id":1818,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6","author":"jemanuel","text":"TEAMS-SPECIFIC TESTING:
- Bot Framework activity handler: verify adaptive card rendering
- Azure AD token refresh: test expired token auto-refresh
- Tenant isolation: verify multi-tenant deployment safety
- @mention parsing: test bot mention extraction from message text
- Channel vs. direct message routing: test both paths
- Teams webhook signature verification (HMAC-SHA256)","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:35Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1","title":"Teams: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix","description":"Define the V3 contract for Teams before implementation expands.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","title":"[T1-MSG.3] fcp.signal — Signal Messenger Connector (via signal-cli bridge)","description":"Signal messenger connector using signal-cli REST API bridge (github.com/bbernhard/signal-cli-rest-api). Privacy-focused messaging for security-conscious users. Operations: signal.send_message (text to individual/group), signal.send_attachment, signal.list_contacts, signal.list_groups, signal.receive_messages (poll for new), signal.trust_identity, signal.get_profile. Auth: signal-cli registered phone number + REST API endpoint. No official bot API — requires self-hosted bridge. Archetype: Polling (receive) + Request-Response (send). Network: configurable bridge URL. Manifest must document bridge prerequisite. 3 subtasks: types/client/error, connector/manifest, tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:55:03.215435Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:25:35.478606Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:55:03.215435Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1784,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7","author":"jemanuel","text":"Testing requirements (per project standard):

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.10","title":"[CROSS] Final Verification Sweep: all 36 connectors pass workspace checks","description":"# Final Verification Sweep

This bead is the campaign-wide closeout gate after the connector expansion lands.

## Goal
Prove that the entire connector expansion is V3-conformant, test-complete, and operationally coherent before the campaign is declared done.

## Required evidence
1. `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` succeeds via `rch`
2. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` succeeds via `rch`
3. `cargo test --workspace` succeeds via `rch`
4. Every new connector demonstrates V3-native `ConnectorRuntime`, `RetryLoop`, `ConnectorErrorMapping`, and typed `OperationInfo`
5. Every manifest passes `fwc manifest fix --check` and expresses prerequisites/network constraints truthfully
6. Every connector has archetype-appropriate unit, integration, and E2E coverage attached to its implementation bead history
7. `fcp-conformance` / `fcp-e2e` evidence is updated where a new archetype or protocol surface required it
8. README and workspace inventory reflect the new connector count accurately
9. Bridge-backed connectors document dedicated environment assumptions and operator remediation paths

## Count correction
The expanded merged roadmap is now 36 new connector features, not 34 or 33. Success means 89 existing connectors + 36 new connectors = 125 total connectors.

## Success criteria
- The campaign can claim V3 connector expansion completeness without caveats
- No connector remains on legacy retry/lifecycle patterns
- No connector remains undocumented about bridge prerequisites, auth models, or network constraints
- Self-hosted messaging additions are verified to the same standard as hosted SaaS +{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.10","title":"[CROSS] Final Verification Sweep: all 36 connectors pass workspace checks","description":"# Final Verification Sweep

This bead is the campaign-wide closeout gate after the connector expansion lands.

## Goal
Prove that the entire connector expansion is V3-conformant, test-complete, and operationally coherent before the campaign is declared done.

## Required evidence
1. `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` succeeds via `rch`
2. `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` succeeds via `rch`
3. `cargo test --workspace` succeeds via `rch`
4. Every new connector demonstrates V3-native `ConnectorRuntime`, `RetryLoop`, `ConnectorErrorMapping`, and typed `OperationInfo`
5. Every manifest passes `fwc manifest fix --check` and expresses prerequisites/network constraints truthfully
6. Every connector has archetype-appropriate unit, integration, and E2E coverage attached to its implementation bead history
7. `fcp-conformance` / `fcp-e2e` evidence is updated where a new archetype or protocol surface required it
8. README and workspace inventory reflect the new connector count accurately
9. Bridge-backed connectors document dedicated environment assumptions and operator remediation paths

## Count correction
The expanded merged roadmap is now 36 new connector features, not 34 or 33. Success means 89 existing connectors + 36 new connectors = 125 total connectors.

## Success criteria
- The campaign can claim V3 connector expansion completeness without caveats
- No connector remains on legacy retry/lifecycle patterns
- No connector remains undocumented about bridge prerequisites, auth models, or network constraints
- Self-hosted messaging additions are verified to the same standard as hosted SaaS  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11","title":"[CROSS-V3] V3 Connector Spec Alignment: acceptance contract, archetype mapping, manifest policy, conformance checklist","description":"# V3 Connector Specification Alignment

## Goal
Create the cross-cutting acceptance contract that every connector-expansion bead inherits, so no future implementation task has to re-derive what “V3-compliant” means from scattered docs.

## Why this exists
The campaign spans radically different archetypes: request-response APIs, webhook-driven messaging, daemon/bridge connectors, database connectors, and multi-service cloud connectors. The V3 protocol invariants must stay stable across all of them:
- `ConnectorRuntime` manages lifecycle and contexts
- `RetryLoop` owns retry behavior
- `ConnectorErrorMapping` defines error translation and retryability
- typed `OperationInfo` carries safety/risk/idempotency semantics
- manifests declare capabilities, network constraints, and prerequisites truthfully
- conformance evidence lives in connector tests plus shared `fcp-conformance` / `fcp-e2e` surfaces

## Outcome
This epic turns that protocol truth into concrete reusable checklists, templates, and dependency anchors for the rest of the campaign.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:27:27.825880Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:27:27.825880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:27:27.825880Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.2","title":"Archetype-specific V3 implementation checklists","description":"Translate the V3 contract into archetype-specific implementation checklists covering the connector families in this campaign: request-response SaaS APIs, webhook/bidirectional messaging connectors, bridge/daemon connectors, database connectors, and multi-service cloud connectors.
